chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي كَمْ يُقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنُ وَ يُخْتَمُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – In how much (time) should the Quran be recited and completed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/504)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/504)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنَ فِي لَيْلَةٍ قَالَ لَا يُعْجِبُنِي أَنْ تَقْرَأَهُ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ شَهْرٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Al Husayn Bin Al Mukhtar, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Can I recite the (whole) Quran in a night?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> do not like it if you were to recite it in less than a month’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Can I read the Holy Quran in one night?’ The Imam said, ‘I do not like your reading it in less than a month’s time.’”
